question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged user submit a ConsensusCreateTopic transaction with an adminKey set to an empty KeyList protobuf, causing createTopic() to call transactionBody.getAdminKey().toByteArray() and store non-null bytes representing an empty key, resulting in a topic where the mirror node reports an adminKey is present but it is semantically empty, misleading consumers about whether the topic is immutable?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged user submit a ConsensusCreateTopic transaction with a feeScheduleKey set to an empty KeyList protobuf, causing createTopic() to call transactionBody.getFeeScheduleKey().toByteArray() and store non-null bytes representing an empty key, resulting in a topic where the mirror node reports a feeScheduleKey is present but it is semantically empty, misleading consumers about fee schedule update authorization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user repeatedly submit ConsensusCreateTopic transactions at high frequency, causing doUpdateEntity() to call entityListener.onEntity() and entityListener.onTopic() for each, flooding the topic and entity tables with large numbers of topic rows and degrading mirror node database performance for all users?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user submit a ConsensusCreateTopic transaction with a customFees list containing entries with a fee collector account ID of 0.0.0, causing updateCustomFee() to call EntityId.of(fixedCustomFee.getFeeCollectorAccountId()) and store a zero/empty collector account, resulting in a custom fee record with an invalid collector that misleads API consumers about fee collection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user submit a ConsensusCreateTopic transaction with a customFees list containing entries with a non-existent denominatingTokenId, causing updateCustomFee() to call EntityId.of(fixedFee.getDenominatingTokenId()) without validating the token's existence and store a dangling token reference, resulting in misleading custom fee data for all API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user submit a ConsensusCreateTopic transaction with a customFees list containing entries with a negative fee amount, causing updateCustomFee() to store a negative amount in the FixedFee without validation, resulting in a custom fee record with a negative amount that misleads API consumers about the topic's fee structure?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user submit a ConsensusCreateTopic transaction with a customFees list containing entries with a fee amount of Long.MAX_VALUE, causing updateCustomFee() to store an extreme amount without overflow checking, resulting in a custom fee record with an overflowed or extreme amount that misleads API consumers about the topic's fee structure?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user submit a ConsensusCreateTopic transaction with a customFees list containing multiple entries with the same collector account and token, causing updateCustomFee() to store duplicate FixedFee entries without deduplication, resulting in inflated custom fee data that misleads API consumers about the actual fee amounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged user submit a ConsensusCreateTopic transaction with a feeExemptKeyList containing keys that do not correspond to any real account on the network, causing createTopic() to serialize and store these phantom keys via addAllKeys() without any existence validation, resulting in a misleading fee-exempt key list that confuses API consumers about who can submit messages fee-free?
